# redpanda_role_binding (Resource)

Binds a role to an account (user or service account), optionally scoped to a specific resource.

Creates a Role Binding in the Redpanda Cloud IAM system.

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `role_name` (String) The name of the role to bind
- `account_id` (String) The ID of the account (user or service account) to bind the role to

### Optional

- `scope_resource_type` (String) The type of resource to scope the role binding to. Valid values: `resource_group`, `network`, `cluster`, `serverless_cluster`, `network_peering`, `organization`. If not set, the role binding is at the organization level.
- `scope_resource_id` (String) The ID of the resource to scope the role binding to. Required when `scope_resource_type` is set.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) UUID of the role binding

## Usage

### Organization-level role binding

```terraform
resource "redpanda_role_binding" "org_admin" {
  role_name  = "admin"
  account_id = "user-abc123"
}
```

### Cluster-scoped role binding

```terraform
resource "redpanda_role_binding" "cluster_editor" {
  role_name           = "editor"
  account_id          = "user-abc123"
  scope_resource_type = "cluster"
  scope_resource_id   = redpanda_cluster.test.id
}
```

### Resource group-scoped role binding

```terraform
resource "redpanda_role_binding" "rg_viewer" {
  role_name           = "viewer"
  account_id          = "service-account-xyz"
  scope_resource_type = "resource_group"
  scope_resource_id   = redpanda_resource_group.test.id
}
```

## Import

```shell
terraform import redpanda_role_binding.example roleBindingId
```
